findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "request_api_key" references sensitive artifacts (api_key). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: Request a free Nefesh API key. No existing API key needed for this call. IMPORTANT: You MUST ask the user for their real email address before calling this tool.…

labels.name: check_api_key_status
labels.description:
Check the status of a pending API key request.

    Use the exact same email the user provided to request_api_key.
    Poll this every 10 seconds. Once the user clicks the verification
    link in their inbox, status changes from 'pending' to 'ready'
    and the response includes the API key. Store

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: request_api_key, check_api_key_status · Private data: request_api_key, check_api_key_status · Public sink: ingest, request_api_key, check_api_key_status.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: request_api_key, check_api_key_status · Private data: request_api_key, check_api_key_status · Public sink: ingest, request_api_key, check_api_key_status

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
